terms.tag: plain English canonical terms. Old jargon lives under `Aliases retired`.

terms.meaning: The cloud scheduler and the process runner that start loops on a timer.
terms.in_our_system: Cloudflare cron dispatches to the Railway loop runner.
terms.is_not: The loop body itself or a one-off script. Is NOT: the product category AI Motor (deterministic execution engine).
terms.example: CF `*/5` cron fires; Railway runs the loop handler.

terms.meaning: Deterministic execution engine. Receives governed execution contracts, orchestrates verified workflows, produces evidence, and returns auditable receipts. Complements LLM reasoning rather than replacing it. Industry: Tesla Vehicle Controller / OpenAI Tool Runtime / Step Functions / Agent Runtime.
terms.in_our_system: product category on /motors/; Shared Runway Motor under runway-core; stack sibling outside Brain — Sensors → Brain → Contract → Kernel → Motor → Evidence → Eval → Learning. Option A LOCKED; Option B (Motor as whole-car container) RETIRED.
terms.is_not: an AI engine; Brain; the whole Tesla-class / governed system car; a chatbot; overnight chat left open; unbounded autonomy; the ops Scheduler and executor alone.
terms.example: An authorized contract reaches Motor; Motor orchestrates verified workflow steps and tools, emits evidence, and returns an auditable receipt.
terms.public_phrasing: AI Motor — deterministic execution engine (not the whole car; not the LLM)
terms.conflict_rule: Never treat AI Motor and AI engine as interchangeable. Never place Brain or AI engines inside Motor. Bare historical code token motor may mean Scheduler and executor — disambiguate by context.
terms.allowed_surfaces: internal, public, website, prompt, contract, receipt

terms.meaning: One intelligence, reasoning, or analysis power unit — a model, critic, classifier, retriever, scorer, draft generator, or decision-support function (Llama / GPT / Gemini / …). Many AI engines live inside Brain.
terms.in_our_system: AI engines live inside Brain only; they answer what should happen / what this means; Motor executes authorized contracts and does not contain Brain or the engine fleet.
terms.is_not: Brain; the AI Motor; the whole product; promotion or deploy authority; continuous case progression by itself.
terms.example: An AI engine inside Brain drafts an STR narrative; Motor executes the authorized follow-on workflow steps and returns a receipt.
terms.public_phrasing: AI engine — intelligence power unit inside Brain (not Motor)
terms.conflict_rule: Prefer AI engine(s) for the intelligence slice inside Brain; never call the whole product or Motor an AI engine; never equate AI engine with Brain.
terms.allowed_surfaces: internal, public, website, prompt, contract, receipt

terms.meaning: Give an agent only the data and permissions required for its task.
terms.in_our_system: Tier 0–2 law routing; workers never load founder judgment patterns.
terms.is_not: Load the whole library so the agent can figure it out.
terms.example: Inbox worker sees queue rows, not contract drafts.
terms.public_phrasing: minimum access required for the task

terms.meaning: Revenue-share or routing percentages spoken only under NDA and in signed contracts.
terms.in_our_system: Contract `[●]` variable only; never public copy.
terms.is_not: A percentage on a website or first-call slide.
terms.example: NDA deck may discuss structure; public pages omit numbers.

terms.meaning: Retired wording. Use canonical term: vendor-neutral.
Retired wording. Use canonical term: vendor-neutral.
Retired wording. Use canonical term: governed reference environment.
terms.conflict_rule: Auto-rewrite to governed reference environment per terminology §6.

terms.meaning: Merriam-Webster's "adversarial" — two sides set up to oppose each other on purpose, like a court's defense vs. prosecution. Applied here: one checker's job is to oppose the work, not agree with it.
terms.is_not: checking your own work · a quick "looks fine" · hostility for its own sake
terms.example: the critique loop's checker attacks the work before it ships; checker is never the same system as the maker
terms.public_phrasing: "checked by a separate reviewer whose job is to look for weaknesses before release"

terms.meaning: IBM's "agentic AI" — software that plans and acts on its own, not just answers when asked — plus FinOps "cost governance" — real budgets and limits on spend, not just a bill you read after. Together: limits placed on AI that acts by itself, so it can't spend unchecked.
terms.is_not: a spending report you read after the money's gone · "we use AI responsibly" with no budget attached · a dashboard with no power to stop anything
terms.example: Tier 1 "AI Spend Leak Audit" finds where autonomous AI spend has no cap, before selling any enforcement

terms.meaning: standard software-pipeline role — collects multiple checkers' outputs and combines them into one verdict.
terms.is_not: a single checker · a vote without a rule for ties
terms.example: IDE lane pipeline combines critic + verifier outputs before a merge decision
terms.public_phrasing: "the step that combines the checks into one result"

terms.meaning: standard security/ops term — noticing when something departs from its normal pattern.
terms.is_not: a person eyeballing a dashboard · a rule that only checks one fixed threshold
terms.example: Line Engine drops a line a gear, or to FROZEN, the moment it detects anomaly

terms.meaning: built on the established term "security theater" (measures that look protective but do nothing) — the opposite discipline: never let a system perform safety without proving it.
terms.is_not: skepticism for its own sake · disbelieving every receipt
terms.example: negative-proof doctrine — a gate isn't trusted until it's shown rejecting a bad input
terms.public_phrasing: "we prove safety, not perform it" Related: child pattern under Pattern Factory.

terms.meaning: Merriam-Webster — one who designs and reviews structure. Here: the role that designs, reviews, and cuts unneeded work, rather than writing features.
terms.is_not: the person who ships code · the founder
terms.example: Architect locks a spec before a worker builds it

terms.meaning: standard software term ("build artifact") — a saved, versioned file a process produces.
terms.is_not: chat scratch · an unsaved draft
terms.example: a signed release file in the library

terms.meaning: Merriam-Webster "audit" (an official examination) + "loop" (a repeating cycle) — a recurring, scheduled examination.
terms.is_not: a one-time look · an internal opinion with no schedule

terms.meaning: a repair pattern that watches system health continuously and fixes known-safe breakages automatically, the way a hospital's standing protocols treat common conditions without waiting for a doctor's fresh judgment each time.
terms.is_not: the same thing as Self-heal (that's the Line Engine's own bounded repair loop) · a fix for anything unknown or unsafe
terms.public_phrasing: "automatic repair of known, safe issues" Related: child pattern under Pattern Factory, distinct from Self-heal (per founder ruling).
terms.related: child pattern under Pattern Factory, distinct from Self-heal (per founder ruling).

terms.meaning: AI-industry standard term — a pretrained model with no added "assistant" instruction-tuning or house style baked in.
terms.is_not: a chat-tuned assistant model · "any small model"
terms.example: the brain runs on a base model specifically so it has no competing definitions of "done" or "verified"

terms.meaning: a base model has no pre-baked definitions of words like "done" or "verified," so our own definitions become its only meaning for those words, instead of competing with a prior meaning. In our system: this is why SSOT and the R1/R2/R6/R7 rules load before anything else — they're not policy sitting beside the model, they're the model's vocabulary.
terms.in_our_system: this is why SSOT and the R1/R2/R6/R7 rules load before anything else — they're not policy sitting beside the model, they're the model's vocabulary.
terms.is_not: true of assistant-tuned models (Cursor, GPT-assistant) — those already carry competing definitions, which is what causes compliance theater
terms.public_phrasing: "we load our own definitions before the model forms any of its own"
terms.banned_surfaces: customer-facing copy (too technical to be useful publicly)
terms.doctrine_source_file: `base-model-first-class-language.md` Public-safe rewrite: "we load our own definitions before the model forms any of its own"

terms.meaning: from Kubernetes' standard "probe" pattern (liveness/readiness checks) — a check that verifies a system actually behaves correctly, not just that it deployed.
terms.is_not: a deploy log · an uptime check with no behavior test
terms.example: Line Engine's mandatory pre-promotion behavior probe

terms.meaning: standard SRE/security term — a cap on how much damage one failure can cause.
terms.is_not: a general "be careful" guideline · unlimited scope with a warning label
terms.example: Line Engine's per-line blast-radius limits, checked every window
terms.public_phrasing: "a limit on how much damage one failure can cause"

terms.meaning: Merriam-Webster — a detailed plan.
terms.is_not: a vague idea · a finished product

terms.meaning: Merriam-Webster "registry" (an official recorded list) applied to blueprints — a governed catalog of approved plans.
terms.is_not: a folder of random files

terms.meaning: our name for a rule-based system that routes and decides using fixed definitions, not guesses.
terms.is_not: a chat assistant · a language model making its own judgment calls
terms.example: the brain classifies intent and picks a route from locked-definitions, never invents a new one
terms.public_phrasing: "a rules-based decision system" — CODE_ALIAS: "Brain" is our product name for it; the plain description above is the real meaning.

terms.meaning: Merriam-Webster "audit" applied to a brain's rules — an examination of whether its decision rules are sound.
terms.is_not: an audit of company finances

terms.meaning: built on the standard industry pattern "X-as-a-Service" (SaaS, PaaS) — the same rules-engine we run internally, offered as a delivered service.
terms.is_not: a chatbot subscription
terms.public_phrasing: "operating brain, installed for you" Related: child pattern under Pattern Factory.

terms.meaning: standard software-resilience term (the "circuit breaker pattern") — stop calling a failing dependency automatically instead of hammering it.
terms.is_not: a manual off switch · a retry loop with no stop condition

terms.meaning: Merriam-Webster — a statement asserting something is true.
terms.is_not: a receipt
terms.example: "Worker said PASS" with no receipt is a claim, not proof
terms.public_phrasing: "an unverified statement" (when no receipt backs it)

terms.meaning: Merriam-Webster "alignment" (agreement in position) applied to business — the contract terms partners actually operate under.
terms.is_not: a free-access request

terms.meaning: a fixed, structured plan format (the Execution Contract) — not the language model — decides what's allowed to run, what output counts as valid, and what it costs. In our system: the model only proposes a plan; it never has authority to execute anything the contract hasn't approved.
terms.in_our_system: the model only proposes a plan; it never has authority to execute anything the contract hasn't approved.
terms.is_not: "the model is in charge" · "we trust the model's judgment at runtime"
terms.public_phrasing: "a fixed set of rules decides what runs — not the AI itself"
terms.banned_surfaces: casual marketing copy (too technical to land as a soundbite)
terms.doctrine_source_file: `contracts-run-the-system.md` Public-safe rewrite: "a fixed set of rules decides what runs — not the AI itself"

terms.meaning: Merriam-Webster "cap" — a fixed upper limit. All work stops once it's hit.
terms.is_not: a soft budget guideline

terms.meaning: standard agentic-AI pipeline role — a component whose only job is to find problems in another component's output.
terms.is_not: the producer reviewing itself

terms.meaning: Merriam-Webster "critique" (a critical evaluation) + "loop" — a recurring, scheduled critical evaluation.
terms.is_not: a one-off comment

terms.meaning: the real, live state of the system, checked directly (an HTTP response, a live page, a database row), not what a commit message or log claims.
terms.is_not: "git push succeeded" · a deploy log
terms.example: CF health check showing 14 live targets vs. 7 in the repo = drift caught by deploy-truth
terms.public_phrasing: "verified live status, not just a deploy log"

terms.meaning: standard startup/enterprise-sales term — an early customer who tests a product in exchange for shaping it, before it's a finished sale.
terms.is_not: a fully committed paying customer

terms.meaning: Merriam-Webster — relating to identifying a problem, not fixing or blocking it.
terms.is_not: "firewall deployed" (that's enforcement) unless a customer receipt proves it
terms.example: Tier 1 "AI Spend Leak Audit" is diagnostic; Tier 3 "Firewall Pilot" is enforcement

terms.meaning: Merriam-Webster — a settled principle or rule. Here: a locked operating rule not re-argued each session.
terms.is_not: a chat opinion · a draft idea

terms.meaning: standard DevOps term "configuration drift" — the live system no longer matching its approved definition.
terms.is_not: "we should improve this someday"
terms.example: a cron schedule that no longer matches the approved registry file
terms.public_phrasing: "mismatch between what's approved and what's actually running"

terms.meaning: Merriam-Webster — to compel compliance, actively.
terms.is_not: describing a policy without applying it
terms.example: pairs with Diagnostic — enforcement requires proof of live installation

terms.meaning: standard incident-management term — a fixed sequence of steps for handling a problem, in order.
terms.is_not: "use the biggest model when unsure"

terms.meaning: standard software/legal term (a binding, structured agreement on what may run) — the fixed schema that decides what a plan is allowed to do before it runs.
terms.is_not: a suggestion the model can reinterpret
terms.public_phrasing: "a fixed rulebook that decides what's allowed to run"

terms.meaning: standard security-engineering term — when a check fails, the system stops, rather than continuing.
terms.is_not: warn-and-continue

terms.meaning: fixed labels in the workflow census: FIX = repair a loop that serves a real user; RETIRE = shut down a loop with no real user.
terms.is_not: a judgment call made loosely

terms.meaning: Merriam-Webster — the person who starts and holds ultimate authority over an organization. Here: Sina, who approves only irreversible actions.
terms.is_not: a manager · an approver of everyday changes

terms.meaning: our own test applied to any proposed rule or process — does it actually serve what the founder is trying to do, or just add process for its own sake.
terms.is_not: a rubber-stamp · a vote

terms.meaning: Merriam-Webster "govern" (to control by rule) — a process the system can stop, measure, and override without asking the agent running it.
terms.is_not: "we use AI responsibly" with no actual stop mechanism
terms.example: a loop with a cost cap, kill switch, and a receipt on every run
terms.public_phrasing: "governed" only if it's actually stoppable and measured — otherwise, downgrade the claim

terms.meaning: a real, inspectable, receipt-backed setup a partner can check directly, instead of a claimed customer list.
terms.is_not: a logo wall · an unprovable "500+ clients" claim

terms.meaning: Merriam-Webster — a central point that other things connect to. Here: the founder's control plane — spawns, pauses, and kills lines; approves only irreversible actions.
terms.is_not: a place that executes work itself

terms.meaning: Merriam-Webster "immutable" (unchangeable) + "floor" (a lower limit) — the safety rules (verifier, kill-switch, gates) that a self-improving system is never allowed to weaken from the inside.
terms.is_not: a suggestion the system can optimize away · a rule the loop can edit if it decides to
terms.example: any change touching a floor path auto-fails and freezes the line — founder-only, out of band
terms.public_phrasing: "the safety rules the system can never weaken on its own"

terms.meaning: standard computer-science term — the core of a system that everything else runs on top of.
terms.is_not: a script that runs once

terms.meaning: standard product/pilot-management term — the conditions, written down before a pilot starts, that will stop it if unmet.
terms.is_not: a judgment made after the fact

terms.meaning: standard safety-engineering term — a mechanism that stops a system immediately.
terms.is_not: a slow manual shutdown process

terms.meaning: Merriam-Webster "lane" (a defined path) — a repo-scoped workstream with its own rules.
terms.is_not: a vague category · the same thing as "Line" (see Line — different concept, cross-referenced)
terms.example: the IDE Lane work order, scoped to one repo

terms.meaning: built on the standard computer-science term "layered architecture" — each agent knows only its own layer and task, not the whole system.
terms.is_not: every agent reading every rule
terms.public_phrasing: "each part of the system only knows its own job"

terms.meaning: Merriam-Webster + standard software term ("code library") — an approved, organized collection.
terms.is_not: a random notes folder

terms.meaning: built on the standard business term "production line" — one full-stack instance (plan → produce → verify → ship → measure), replicated per revenue vertical.
terms.is_not: the same thing as "Lane" (a repo workstream — different concept, cross-referenced)
terms.example: distribution line, factory line, TrustField line

terms.meaning: plain-English combination — a signal read directly from the running system right now, not from a file.
terms.is_not: a status a file claims
terms.example: motor/loop health check reads receipts newer than twice the loop interval

terms.meaning: our own file of approved definitions the brain is allowed to use — a small subset of the full dictionary.
terms.is_not: the full dictionary loaded into every worker

terms.meaning: Merriam-Webster + standard software term ("control loop") — a recurring task on a schedule, with a cost cap, a kill path, and a receipt per run.
terms.is_not: unbounded "always-on" · a Cursor session left open

terms.meaning: a safety rule only counts if a machine enforces it (a hook, a CI check, code) — not if it's just written instructions an agent is asked to follow. In our system: the test is "can an agent satisfy this by producing text, without the real condition being true?" If yes, it's prose and must be rewritten as code.
terms.in_our_system: the test is "can an agent satisfy this by producing text, without the real condition being true?" If yes, it's prose and must be rewritten as code.
terms.is_not: "we documented the rule clearly" · a policy an agent is trusted to self-apply
terms.example: `P7-DOCTRINE/mechanical-not-prose.md` — the `.agent-policy/` directory is hook-rejected on every agent lane
terms.public_phrasing: "our safety rules are enforced by code, not just written down"
terms.banned_surfaces: none — safe to say publicly in plain form
terms.doctrine_source_file: `mechanical-not-prose.md` Public-safe rewrite: "our safety rules are enforced by code, not just written down"

terms.meaning: standard software/git-review term — the permission level required before a change can be merged.
terms.is_not: one blanket permission for all changes
terms.example: `P8-MACHINE-LOOPS/merge-authority-tiers-T0-T3.md` — T0 docs/tests merge by machine, T3 schema/gates require the founder
terms.public_phrasing: "graduated approval, based on risk" `CODE_ALIAS`: the T0–T3 tier labels themselves are literal gate names in code.

terms.meaning: standard legal term — Non-Disclosure Agreement, a confidentiality contract signed before sharing sensitive information.
terms.is_not: a verbal understanding

terms.meaning: built on the standard QA term "negative testing" (proving a system correctly rejects bad input) — a gate is only proven by showing it reject something it should reject, not by a description claiming it works.
terms.is_not: prose asserting a gate works · a gate that has only ever seen passing input
terms.example: `P7-DOCTRINE/negative-proof.md` — a deliberately-failing test commit the gate must reject, captured as a receipt
terms.public_phrasing: "we prove our checks by testing what they correctly reject"

terms.meaning: Merriam-Webster "observation" (a noted, factual account) + "record" — a timestamped status snapshot, not full proof of payment or outcome.
terms.is_not: R≥1 proof · a substitute for a receipt on revenue claims

terms.meaning: our own name for the parent thesis — every repeatable pattern the system runs on: healing, improvement, commercial, cost, buyer-signal, workflow-failure, or any other repeatable world/system pattern.
terms.is_not: the same thing as any one of its child patterns (Self-heal, Auto-heal, Signal Factory, Anti-theater, Brain-as-a-service are children, not the whole)

terms.meaning: our own name for the fixed schema an LLM's output must follow before it can run — the only shape a plan is allowed to take.
terms.is_not: free-form model output

terms.meaning: standard agentic-AI pipeline role — breaks a goal into an ordered set of steps.
terms.is_not: the component that executes the steps
terms.public_phrasing: "the step that breaks a goal into an ordered plan"

terms.meaning: standard distributed-systems term (the Kubernetes "reconciliation" pattern) — the one component that resolves conflicts and decides the final state.
terms.is_not: any component that merely touches state

terms.meaning: Merriam-Webster "repair" + "loop" — a recurring, scheduled fix cycle.
terms.is_not: a one-time manual fix

terms.meaning: Merriam-Webster "research" + "loop" — a recurring, scheduled research cycle.
terms.is_not: a one-off search

terms.meaning: our own name for a producer role that gathers outside information (e.g. via Perplexity or computer-use) to feed a line.
terms.is_not: the AI Stations (specialist producer roles that create content, not gather it — a related but separate role)

terms.meaning: our own term modeled on the standard business metric "return on investment" — applied to thinking/work instead of money: is this the highest-value use of effort right now, or should it be removed.
terms.is_not: "we're always busy" as a justification

terms.meaning: standard business term "ROI" (return on investment) + "engine" (a processing system) — the component that ranks lines by real return.
terms.is_not: a vanity metrics dashboard
terms.public_phrasing: "the system that ranks by real return"

terms.meaning: standard software term — a dispatcher that sends work to the right destination based on rules, not a decision-maker itself.
terms.is_not: "the brain" · a component that makes judgment calls
terms.example: capability router + circuit breaker, reclassified explicitly as dispatcher not brain

terms.meaning: our own shorthand — the first receipt of real payment from an external user.
terms.is_not: an internal test transaction · founder self-pay

terms.meaning: our own name for the one approved file that stores the schedule every live cron job must match exactly.
terms.is_not: any config file that happens to have a schedule in it

terms.meaning: Merriam-Webster — choosing carefully among options, working with only a few partners chosen on evidence.
terms.is_not: snobbery · an unprovable "elite" claim

terms.meaning: our own name for the Line Engine's own bounded repair loop — fixes a line automatically, within a fixed scope, requiring real signal before improving anything.
terms.is_not: the same thing as Auto-heal (that's the broader Pattern Factory hospital-policy pattern) · an unbounded ability to change anything

terms.meaning: our own name for the applied skill that triages any raw inbound message into a fixed decision report — classification, need, four scores, one next action, a receipt.
terms.is_not: the same thing as Pattern Factory (Signal Factory is one applied child pattern, not the parent thesis)
terms.example: `P9-PATTERN-FACTORY/signal-factory-v1.md` — implemented as the installed signal-factory skill

terms.meaning: our own metaphor — "soup" = probabilistic model output carrying its own invented meaning; "raw" = a base model with no competing definitions, running on our fixed rules.
terms.is_not: a claim that any model is bad

terms.meaning: Merriam-Webster — someone or something scoped to one narrow task.
terms.is_not: a general-purpose worker with no scope limit
terms.example: AI Stations, each an adversarial specialist role

terms.meaning: standard distributed-systems term — two live instances of a system both acting as the authoritative source at once.
terms.is_not: two backups, one of which is clearly inactive
terms.public_phrasing: "two systems disagreeing about which one is authoritative"

terms.meaning: standard data-governance term "single source of truth" — the one authoritative file or record when copies disagree.
terms.is_not: the file that happens to be open right now

terms.meaning: a confidential business arrangement broader than a simple revenue-share — covers any structured commercial term (not only revenue splits) that stays private between the parties.
terms.is_not: only a revenue-sharing agreement · public pricing
terms.public_phrasing: not public-facing, ever, in specific form `[NEEDS ARCHITECT]`: exact boundary of "broader than revenue share" not yet spelled out in any doc — flagging for a sharper definition when one is written.

terms.meaning: Merriam-Webster (an underlying layer) + standard tech usage ("compute substrate") — the shared safety floor every line stands on.
terms.is_not: a line itself · something a line can bypass

terms.meaning: our own name for the rule that the Hub can only trigger irreversible actions manually (a tap), never run them automatically.
terms.is_not: full automation of irreversible actions

terms.meaning: Merriam-Webster "target" (a goal) + standard project-management term "blocker" (something stopping work) — only real damage should stop work; smaller issues get improved next cycle, not used as an excuse to freeze.
terms.is_not: an excuse to ignore real problems
terms.public_phrasing: "we keep moving on targets, stop only for real blockers"

terms.meaning: standard support-tiering pattern, applied to our own offers — Tier 1: AI Spend Leak Audit (diagnostic). Tier 2: Cost Policy Pack. Tier 3: Model Firewall Pilot (enforcement, receipt-gated).
terms.is_not: interchangeable — Tier 3 enforcement claims require a customer receipt, per policy rule 4

terms.meaning: standard distributed-database term (Cassandra and similar systems mark deleted rows with a "tombstone") — a marker that flags an item as dead so it's never used again.
terms.is_not: simply deleting a file with no record

terms.meaning: Separate venture for regulated financial workflows (Canada). Name reserved with BC Registries — not incorporated, not filed. Noetfield/SourceA may serve it; never a Noetfield product or subsidiary.
Merriam-Webster "validate" + "loop" — a recurring, scheduled correctness check.
terms.is_not: a one-time test run

terms.meaning: standard business/tech term — using no default AI provider, routing work by receipts, cancelable at any time.
terms.is_not: "agnostic" in customer-facing text · a refusal to ever partner

terms.meaning: Merriam-Webster "verify" + standard agentic-AI pipeline role — an independent component that checks correctness, separate from the one that produced the work.
terms.is_not: the producer checking itself

terms.meaning: Merriam-Webster — one who performs a task. Here: an LLM or coding agent that drafts or runs bounded work, without deciding strategy.
terms.is_not: a decision-maker · the architect

terms.meaning: our own name for keeping the language of the system stable over time — connects directly to Drift, applied specifically to what words mean instead of what code runs.
terms.is_not: the same as system-health drift (Auto-heal handles that) — this is about language specifically
terms.public_phrasing: "we keep our language consistent over time" Related: child pattern under Pattern Factory.

terms.meaning: our own term — the system proves its own state with receipts, without needing the founder to manually confirm it.
terms.is_not: the founder being cut out of irreversible decisions — those still require the founder

terms.meaning: GitHub Actions run UI — invalid external-verify path; building agent cannot treat "check Actions UI" as PASS.
Tier-1 deliverable: policy templates and spend-guard recommendations from diagnostic engagement.
Tier-1 diagnostic SKU: find autonomous AI spend paths with no cap, owner, or receipt.
Execute scoped change only in sina-governance-ssot — governance registry, gate, validators.
Stable identifier for an authority document, role, or merge tier.
Layer where merge authority, lane law, and irreversible founder gates are enforced.
Shorthand for census class GUARD — loop exists to verify, probe, or audit system integrity.
FinOps discipline for automation spend: caps, receipts, and kill paths per loop.
NOOS/GHA factory autorun lane — META census candidate; not revenue path.
Brain-as-a-Service tier gated: proof ladder milestone not met; do not sell as proven.
Minimal deterministic brain install — Tier 2 path after one SourceA pattern proof.
Production brain worker with live health endpoints; deploy-truth required before claims.
Runtime agent attached to deterministic brain — drafts and executes under gates; does not own meaning.
Structure: deterministic core, walled LLM workers, gates, receipts — not a single model.
Diagnostic engagement family: drift, theater, and coherence review before Operating Brain Install.
Tier-1 deliverable summarizing semantic and policy coherence findings — diagnostic only.
Approved public claims the brain may state — P6 scope, not full dictionary.
Informal name for full operating-brain stack; use locked SKUs in contracts.
Founder-locked bootstrap rules for brain registry — not default worker load.
Role that ships bounded change under gates — not the decider.
Named milestone in phase-loop build plan — scoped delivery slice.
Stand up minimum TrustField product slice in trustfield-loops — separate from SourceA brain.
Approved go-to-market narrative tied to proof ladder and R≥1 — not ad-hoc pitch.
Canonical chat migration product (v4.9.9) — copy-first migration path.
Cloudflare motor cf_loop_specialist_tick_v1 for loop_specialist_runtime_plan.
GitHub Copilot assist-only quick scan lane — draft PRs, no autonomous merge.
Repo posture for Copilot assist_only workflows and PR template compliance.
Work item to open TrustField venture lane: entity hygiene, sandbox, regulated scope.
Factory mesh graph linking nodes, edges, and receipt consumers.
Checkpoint requiring explicit PASS before next phase or promote.
Founder/doc step locking first TrustField MVP scope before build spend.
Align committed disk state with cloud kernel truth — fail-closed on drift.
Per-domain authority files; on conflict the SSOT row wins.
Registry of eval passes, promotion gates, and rollback posture.
Iterate SourceA factory/build executor — internal lane slug.
E2E gate validating contract pages and execution paths against SSOT.
SourceA cloud forge automation surface — bounded build/deploy.
Meta-label in DLM triage for form option chrome (Nov Options, PICKs Options) — not system vocabulary.
Outbound email motion — founder-execute until stranger payment receipt.
